key_insight: High-velocity air-fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that still retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This enables functional NiTi coatings on steel components for adaptive surface protection, actuation, and wear-resistant smart surfaces.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four process parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Post-annealing was used to recover/activate shape-memory behavior in the coatings
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by microscopy and phase/functional tests, and they showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation in 3-point bending and shape-memory response in nanoindentation and scratch tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings were microstructurally inhomogeneous with voids, oxides, and internal stress, so annealing and process control are critical to achieve reliable performance.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
